title: cheilanthes fragrans subsp. maderensis
intro: Cheilanthes fragrans subsp. maderensis, also known as Madeiran lip fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Aspleniaceae, commonly found in the Atlantic Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Cheilanthes fragrans subsp. maderensis is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a dried extract, tea, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
